operationIdentifier.description: The ID of the asynchronous operation, which is used to track status. The operation is available for 90 days.

controlOperations.description: Returns a list of output from control operations.

startTime.description: The time at which a control operation began.

endTime.description: The time at which the control operation was completed.

status.description: The status of the specified control operation.

statusMessage.description: A speficic message displayed as part of the control status.

operationIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of a control operation.

targetIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of the target of a control operation.

enabledBaselines.description: Retuens a list of summaries of EnabledBaseline resources.

baselineIdentifier.description: The specific baseline that is enabled as part of the EnabledBaseline resource.

driftStatusSummary.description: The drift status of the enabled baseline.

types.description: The types of drift that can be detected for an enabled baseline. Amazon Web Services Control Tower detects inheritance drift on enabled baselines that apply at the OU level.

inheritance.description: At least one account within the target OU does not match the baseline configuration defined on that OU. An account is in inheritance drift when it does not match the configuration of a parent OU, possibly a new parent OU, if the account is moved.

status.description: The inheritance drift status for enabled baselines.

targetIdentifier.description: The target upon which the baseline is enabled.

parentIdentifier.description: An ARN that represents an object returned by ListEnabledBaseline, to describe an enabled baseline.

statusSummary.description: The deployment summary of an EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline resource.

status.description:
The deployment status of the enabled resource.

Valid values:

  • SUCCEEDED: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration was deployed successfully.

  • UNDER_CHANGE: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration is changing.

  • FAILED: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration failed to deploy.

lastOperationIdentifier.description: The last operation identifier for the enabled resource.

enabledControls.description: Lists the controls enabled by Amazon Web Services Control Tower on the specified organizational unit and the accounts it contains.

nextToken.description: Retrieves the next page of results. If the string is empty, the response is the end of the results.

statusSummary.description: A short description of the status of the enabled control.

status.description:
The deployment status of the enabled resource.

Valid values:

  • SUCCEEDED: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration was deployed successfully.

  • UNDER_CHANGE: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration is changing.

  • FAILED: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration failed to deploy.

lastOperationIdentifier.description: The last operation identifier for the enabled resource.

driftStatusSummary.description: The drift status of the enabled control.

driftStatus.description:
The drift status of the enabled control.

Valid values:

  • DRIFTED: The enabledControl deployed in this configuration doesn’t match the configuration that Amazon Web Services Control Tower expected.

  • IN_SYNC: The enabledControl deployed in this configuration matches the configuration that Amazon Web Services Control Tower expected.

  • NOT_CHECKING: Amazon Web Services Control Tower does not check drift for this enabled control. Drift is not supported for the control type.

  • UNKNOWN: Amazon Web Services Control Tower is not able to check the drift status for the enabled control.

types.description: An object that categorizes the different types of drift detected for the enabled control.

inheritance.description: Indicates drift related to inheritance configuration between parent and child controls.

status.description: The status of inheritance drift for the enabled control, indicating whether inheritance configuration matches expectations.

resource.description: Indicates drift related to the underlying Amazon Web Services resources managed by the control.

status.description: The status of resource drift for the enabled control, indicating whether the underlying resources match the expected configuration.

parentIdentifier.description: The ARN of the parent enabled control from which this control inherits its configuration, if applicable.

nextToken.description: Retrieves the next page of results. If the string is empty, the response is the end of the results.

operationType.description: The type of the landing zone operation.

status.description: The status of the landing zone operation.

nextToken.description: Retrieves the next page of results. If the string is empty, the response is the end of the results.

arn.description: The ARN of the landing zone resource.

operationIdentifier.description: A unique identifier assigned to a CreateLandingZone operation. You can use this identifier as an input of GetLandingZoneOperation to check the operation's status.
>A unique identifier assigned to a DeleteLandingZone operation. You can use this identifier as an input parameter of GetLandingZoneOperation to check the operation's status.
The ID (in UUID format) of the asynchronous DisableBaseline operation. This operationIdentifier is used to track status through calls to the GetBaselineOperation API.
The ID (in UUID format) of the asynchronous EnableBaseline operation. This operationIdentifier is used to track status through calls to the GetBaselineOperation API.
The ID of the asynchronous operation, which is used to track status. The operation is available for 90 days.

baselineOperation.description: A baselineOperation object that shows information about the specified operation ID.

operationType.description: An enumerated type (enum) with possible values of ENABLE_BASELINE, DISABLE_BASELINE, UPDATE_ENABLED_BASELINE, or RESET_ENABLED_BASELINE.

status.description: An enumerated type (enum) with possible values of SUCCEEDED, FAILED, or IN_PROGRESS.

startTime.description: The start time of the operation, in ISO 8601 format.

endTime.description: The end time of the operation (if applicable), in ISO 8601 format.

statusMessage.description: A status message that gives more information about the operation's status, if applicable.
If the operation result is FAILED, this string contains a message explaining why the operation failed.

controlIdentifier.description: The controlIdentifier of the control for the operation.

targetIdentifier.description: The target upon which the control operation is working.

baselineIdentifier.description: The specific Baseline enabled as part of the EnabledBaseline resource.

driftStatusSummary.description: The drift status of the enabled baseline.

types.description: The types of drift that can be detected for an enabled baseline. Amazon Web Services Control Tower detects inheritance drift on enabled baselines that apply at the OU level.

inheritance.description: At least one account within the target OU does not match the baseline configuration defined on that OU. An account is in inheritance drift when it does not match the configuration of a parent OU, possibly a new parent OU, if the account is moved.

status.description: The inheritance drift status for enabled baselines.

targetIdentifier.description: The target on which to enable the Baseline.

parentIdentifier.description: An ARN that represents the parent EnabledBaseline at the Organizational Unit (OU) level, from which the child EnabledBaseline inherits its configuration. The value is returned by GetEnabledBaseline.

statusSummary.description: The deployment summary of an EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline resource.

status.description:
The deployment status of the enabled resource.

Valid values:

  • SUCCEEDED: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration was deployed successfully.

  • UNDER_CHANGE: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration is changing.

  • FAILED: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration failed to deploy.

lastOperationIdentifier.description: The last operation identifier for the enabled resource.

parameters.description: Shows the parameters that are applied when enabling this Baseline.

controlIdentifier.description: The control identifier of the enabled control. For information on how to find the controlIdentifier, see the overview page.

targetIdentifier.description: The ARN of the organizational unit. For information on how to find the targetIdentifier, see the overview page.

statusSummary.description: The deployment summary of the enabled control.

status.description:
The deployment status of the enabled resource.

  • SUCCEEDED: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration was deployed successfully.

  • UNDER_CHANGE: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration is changing.

  • FAILED: The EnabledControl or EnabledBaseline configuration failed to deploy.

lastOperationIdentifier.description: The last operation identifier for the enabled resource.

driftStatusSummary.description: The drift status of the enabled control.

driftStatus.description:
The drift status of the enabled control.

Valid values:

  • DRIFTED: The enabledControl deployed in this configuration doesn’t match the configuration that Amazon Web Services Control Tower expected.

  • IN_SYNC: The enabledControl deployed in this configuration matches the configuration that Amazon Web Services Control Tower expected.

  • NOT_CHECKING: Amazon Web Services Control Tower does not check drift for this enabled control. Drift is not supported for the control type.

  • UNKNOWN: Amazon Web Services Control Tower is not able to check the drift status for the enabled control.

types.description: An object that categorizes the different types of drift detected for the enabled control.

inheritance.description: Indicates drift related to inheritance configuration between parent and child controls.

status.description: The status of inheritance drift for the enabled control, indicating whether inheritance configuration matches expectations.

resource.description: Indicates drift related to the underlying Amazon Web Services resources managed by the control.

status.description: The status of resource drift for the enabled control, indicating whether the underlying resources match the expected configuration.

parentIdentifier.description: The ARN of the parent enabled control from which this control inherits its configuration, if applicable.

targetRegions.description: Target Amazon Web Services Regions for the enabled control.

remediationTypes.description: The types of remediation actions configured for the landing zone, such as automatic drift correction or compliance enforcement.

status.description: The landing zone deployment status. One of ACTIVE, PROCESSING, FAILED.

latestAvailableVersion.description: The latest available version of the landing zone.

driftStatus.description: The drift status of the landing zone.

status.description:
The drift status of the landing zone.

Valid values:

  • DRIFTED: The landing zone deployed in this configuration does not match the configuration that Amazon Web Services Control Tower expected.

  • IN_SYNC: The landing zone deployed in this configuration matches the configuration that Amazon Web Services Control Tower expected.

manifest.description: The landing zone manifest JSON text file that specifies the landing zone configurations.

operationType.description:
The landing zone operation type.

Valid values:

  • DELETE: The DeleteLandingZone operation.

  • CREATE: The CreateLandingZone operation.

  • UPDATE: The UpdateLandingZone operation.

  • RESET: The ResetLandingZone operation.

status.description:
Valid values:

  • SUCCEEDED: The landing zone operation succeeded.

  • IN_PROGRESS: The landing zone operation is in progress.

  • FAILED: The landing zone operation failed.

statusMessage.description: If the operation result is FAILED, this string contains a message explaining why the operation failed.

operationIdentifier.description: The ID (in UUID format) of the asynchronous ResetEnabledBaseline operation. This operationIdentifier is used to track status through calls to the GetBaselineOperation API.
The operation identifier for this ResetEnabledControl operation.
A unique identifier assigned to a ResetLandingZone operation. You can use this identifier as an input parameter of GetLandingZoneOperation to check the operation's status.
The ID (in UUID format) of the asynchronous UpdateEnabledBaseline operation. This operationIdentifier is used to track status through calls to the GetBaselineOperation API.
The operation identifier for this UpdateEnabledControl operation.
A unique identifier assigned to a UpdateLandingZone operation. You can use this identifier as an input of GetLandingZoneOperation to check the operation's status.
